name: Manoa Falls Trail
tagline: A short, shaded rainforest hike to a 150-foot waterfall — doable for most keiki and absolutely magical.
parking: $7 paid parking lot at trailhead; fills up by 10am on weekends
hours: Open sunrise to sunset daily; gate closes at 6pm
bestTime: Early morning on weekdays; trail gets muddy after rain but the waterfall is more impressive
talkStory: Good for ages 4 and up (or carry younger ones). The trail is 1.6 miles round trip through a bamboo and banyan rainforest — feels like Jurassic Park. It's muddy, so wear shoes you don't mind getting dirty. Mosquito repellent is essential. Do not swim in the pool at the base — leptospirosis risk. Bring a light rain jacket. The Lyon Arboretum next door is a nice add-on if kids have energy left.
